evals.name: investigates-before-fixing
evals.prompt: The /api/users endpoint returns 500 with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading map)'. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads the error, finds the file, traces the data flow to identify WHERE the undefined value comes from — does NOT propose a fix before understanding the root cause

assertions.text: Reads the error message carefully
Reads the source file before proposing fix
Traces the data flow (checks what calls this function)
Does NOT propose a fix in the first response
States a hypothesis before fixing

evals.name: checks-recent-changes
evals.prompt: The login page stopped working after today's deployment. Users get a blank white page. It was fine yesterday.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks git log for recent changes, identifies what changed between yesterday and today, correlates changes with the symptom

assertions.text: Checks git log or recent commits
Identifies what changed recently
Correlates changes with the symptom
Doesn't immediately propose a rollback without understanding

evals.name: reproduces-before-fixing
evals.prompt: Users report that the search feature 'sometimes returns wrong results'. I can't figure out when it fails. Help me debug it.
evals.expected_output: Agent tries to reproduce consistently — asks about specific inputs, checks for pattern (timing, data, user), doesn't guess at a fix

assertions.text: Asks about reproduction steps
Looks for patterns
Doesn't guess at a fix without reproduction

evals.name: single-hypothesis-minimal-change
evals.prompt: I found the bug: the date parsing function crashes on dates formatted as DD/MM/YYYY but works for MM/DD/YYYY. The function uses new Date(input). Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Agent forms a clear hypothesis (Date constructor interprets DD/MM as MM/DD), makes ONE minimal change to fix the parsing, doesn't refactor the entire date handling system

assertions.text: States the hypothesis clearly
Makes a single minimal fix
Does NOT over-engineer (no date library, no rewrite)
Suggests verifying the fix with a test

evals.name: stops-after-three-failed-fixes
evals.prompt: I've already tried 3 things to fix this flaky test: (1) added a sleep, (2) increased the timeout, (3) wrapped it in a retry loop. None worked — it still fails about 10% of the time. What should I try next?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes the 3-fix threshold, questions whether the approach/architecture is wrong instead of proposing fix #4, suggests investigating the root cause of the flakiness

assertions.text: Recognizes the pattern of repeated failed fixes
Questions the architecture/approach
Does NOT propose another band-aid fix
Suggests going back to investigation
